is an American tech [entrepreneur](/wiki/Entrepreneur "Entrepreneur") and [software developer](/wiki/Software_development "Software development"), who is known for co-founding the social media platform [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") and [Odeo](/wiki/Odeo "Odeo"), a [podcasting](/wiki/Podcasting "Podcasting") company that shut down in 2017.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Glass is credited for coining the name "Twitter", which began as "Twttr".[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American technology entrepreneur

For the founder of the digital ordering software Olo, see [Olo (online ordering)](/wiki/Olo_(online_ordering) "Olo (online ordering)").

| Noah Glass | |
| --- | --- |
| Glass in 2007 | |
| Occupation | Businessperson |
| Known for | Co-founded [Odeo](/wiki/Odeo "Odeo") and [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") |

**Noah Glass** is an American tech [entrepreneur](/wiki/Entrepreneur "Entrepreneur") and [software developer](/wiki/Software_development "Software development"), who is known for co-founding the social media platform [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter") and [Odeo](/wiki/Odeo "Odeo"), a [podcasting](/wiki/Podcasting "Podcasting") company that shut down in 2017.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) Glass is credited for coining the name "Twitter", which began as "Twttr".[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Noah_Glass&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

After leaving [Industrial Light & Magic](/wiki/Industrial_Light_%26_Magic "Industrial Light & Magic"), Glass worked on several projects with [Marc Canter](/wiki/Marc_Canter "Marc Canter"), founder of [MacroMind](/wiki/MacroMind "MacroMind") which later became [Macromedia](/wiki/Macromedia "Macromedia"), birthplace of the [Shockwave](/wiki/Adobe_Shockwave "Adobe Shockwave") [multimedia](/wiki/Multimedia "Multimedia") [platform](/wiki/Platform_(computing) "Platform (computing)").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

He later developed an app that allowed a user to enter an [audio blog](/wiki/Podcast "Podcast") entry from a remote cell phone location. His small start-up, known as AudBlog, was eventually folded into a partnership with [Evan Williams](/wiki/Evan_Williams_(Internet_entrepreneur) "Evan Williams (Internet entrepreneur)"), of [Blogger](/wiki/Blogger_(service) "Blogger (service)"). The duo then created [Odeo](/wiki/Odeo "Odeo"), a podcasting company.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[4]](#cite_note-:0-4)

In 2006, while with Odeo, Glass helped to create and develop the seed idea for what would eventually become known as [Twitter](/wiki/Twitter "Twitter"), and he is acknowledged as being responsible for coining the name "Twitter", which began as the [abbreviated](/wiki/Abbreviated "Abbreviated") version, "Twttr".[[7]](#cite_note-7) In the book, *Hatching Twitter*, by [Nick Bilton](/wiki/Nick_Bilton "Nick Bilton"), Glass is given credit as being a Twitter co-founder,[[8]](#cite_note-8) having helped realize the idea, and designing some of its core features.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)
